purpose: Define the canonical contract for the master module registry so every module can be coupled, decoupled, replaced, paused and resumed without touching the frozen core.
principles: Controlled lifecycle with human evidence when needed
No external network and no Git write by default

included: Operational memory for the module set

registry_behavior.coupling_model: Modules must declare identity, version, state, dependencies and compatibility before activation.
registry_behavior.decoupling_model: Any module can be detached by policy without breaking the registry contract.
registry_behavior.replacement_model: A module can be replaced only through explicit compatibility and lifecycle transition.
registry_behavior.pause_model: Paused modules remain visible, traceable and recoverable.
registry_behavior.memory_model: The registry keeps operational memory of current, paused, deprecated and retired modules.

acceptance_criteria: The registry is deterministic and local-first.
The registry supports coupling and decoupling without core changes.
Every module has identity, version, state and dependencies.
Lifecycle changes are explicit and traceable.
The dashboard shows the registry status and the lifecycle model.

validation.method: Local JSON parsing plus file existence checks.
